title: General or reserved exceptions should never be thrown
description: This rule raises an issue when a general or reserved exception is thrown. Throwing general exceptions such as Exception, SystemException and ApplicationException will have a negative impact on any code trying to catch these exceptions. From a consumer perspective, it is generally a best practice to only catch exceptions you intend to handle.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects throwing of general or reserved exceptions (Exception, SystemException, ApplicationException)
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when: throw new Exception, SystemException, or ApplicationException is found
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: specific exception types, comments, test code
logic.reasoning: Throwing general exceptions like Exception, SystemException, or ApplicationException makes it hard for consumers to handle specific error cases appropriately. This rule detects 'throw new' statements followed by these general exception types. Pattern matches 'throw new' followed by 'Exception', 'SystemException', or 'ApplicationException' with word boundaries to avoid matching specific exceptions like ArgumentException.
